host: i7-11800H (16T) / RTX 3060 Laptop 6 GiB (GA106, CC 8.6) / Win11 / driver 576.83 / CUDA 12.9 / WDDM

optimization.name: uint4 f16 K-cache read (attention decode)
optimization.change: widen scalar u16 f16 K read to uint4 (128-bit, 8 keys/load), same d-order accumulation
optimization.byte_identical_reason: fmad=false + preserved operation order => same bits; alignment exact (head_dim%8==0 -> 16B-aligned rows; guard falls back to scalar otherwise)
optimization.designed_by: 10-agent design workflow (4 proposals -> judge -> 4 adversarial refuters); chose uint4 byte-identical read

correctness.splitk_spec_verify_bit_identical: PASS (ran 1.44s, not skipped; asserts decode == attention_batched == attention_tree_batched bit-identical, pc 512..4096)
correctness.implication: decode == spec-verify bit-identical => LOSSLESS SPECULATIVE DECODE PRESERVED (the property CAMELID_ATTN_COALESCED breaks)
correctness.G_ge_2_coverage: test exercises attention_decode at pc=512/G=4 (closes the runtime-probe G=1 blind spot the adversarial reviewer flagged)

verdict: STRICTLY DOMINATES the coalesced kernel: faster AND byte-identical (preserves lossless spec-decode). Ship default-on; recommend deprecating CAMELID_ATTN_COALESCED.
followups: uint4 weighted-V read (needs mandatory G>=2 device test, NOT the runtime probe)
